examples: Several corridors, most of them were half decayed, led finally to a chamber with a heavy iron door.
If the body has only partially decayed, it is reburied in a smaller cheaper grave, but not for long, the body will be dug up again some time later when it has fully decayed.
Even young children have teeth so decayed they need to be pulled.
And over time, if traditional Medicare has decayed or fallen apart, then they're stuck.
She praised the HP Way to the ranks but felt that it had decayed into consensus management.
